title: Install Playwright + browsers, browser-verify synths audio engine
description: npm ci (lockfile-pinned @playwright/test, supply-chain exempt) + npx playwright install chromium firefox on hosaka. Then verify synths.html audio: each era 'play this sound' triggers sound on gesture, transport plays in sync, no console errors. Blocks closing c61.3–c61.6 (engine code written + node syntax-checked, but NOT yet verified in a real browser). hosaka is headless — Playwright is the verification path.

title: Remove unused pymdown-extensions from requirements.txt
description: site/requirements.txt:2 pins pymdown-extensions>=10.0 but build.py:18 uses MD_EXTENSIONS=['tables','fenced_code','toc','meta'] — all stdlib. No code path imports pymdownx. Delete the line to stop pulling an unused dependency into fresh pip installs.

title: tb303.html compounds DPR transform on every resize
description: site/static/tb303.html:766 calls ctx.scale(dpr, dpr) inside resize() which is fired repeatedly during window drag / DPR change. Transform multiplies each call — after a single drag sequence rendering can shrink or grow. Mirrorball does this correctly with ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0) at mirrorball.html:451. Replace scale() with setTransform().

title: Missing favicon.ico — 404 on every retro-themed page
description: site/templates/base.html.j2:11 references /img/favicon.ico but site/static/img/ is empty. Every retro page (home, research/*) logs a 404 network request. Canvas pages (index.html, mirrorball.html, tb303.html) also have no favicon link and inherit the browser's auto /favicon.ico probe. Fix: add a data-URI SVG favicon link to base.html.j2 AND to the three standalone static HTML files. Suggest a smiley glyph to match the new particle direction.

title: Move ctx.filter blurs into offscreen canvases (mirrorball perf)
description: Firefox tanks to ~15fps because three full-canvas blur passes (bloom) plus the haze composite blur run via ctx.filter at full W×H every frame. Firefox's Canvas2D filter is CPU-bound; Chrome runs it on GPU. The choppy reflected-dot animation is purely a framerate symptom (rotation = ω·dt, low fps = large angular jumps). Chrome's cotton-ball smoke is the inverse problem: Chrome uses a fast box-blur approximation that doesn't smear the 80 underlying haze particles. Move blurs into the small offscreen canvases (1/4 res haze, 1/8 res bloom) using a sibling temp canvas, then drawImage unfiltered. Pixel cost drops 16-64x, and the bilinear upscale on already-blurred small images smooths the discrete circles.

title: Decide SD×Canvas direction from A/B/C review
description: User reviews the three passes and picks the integration approach (or hybrid). Output: chosen direction recorded, feeds the real asset pipeline + c61.8 Visuals.

title: Screenshot A/B/C passes (Playwright) + share for direction decision
description: Once A/B/C mockups exist, capture screenshots (and/or serve via mesh URL hosaka.personal.internal) so the user can compare and choose the SD×Canvas direction. Blocks the direction decision.

title: Pass C mockup: comic / motion panels revealed on scroll
description: Quick pass of approach C. Placeholder comic panels (rects/scenes) revealed in sequence on scroll with canvas reactive accents/morph between panels. Conveys the storytelling-heavy, asset-heavy option for comparison.

title: Pass B mockup: consistent-character journey + canvas signal-flow overlay
description: Quick pass of approach B. Approximate a recurring figure/machine evolving across the 5 eras (placeholder silhouette/shape per era since no real SD frames yet), with the canvas signal-flow/audio overlay on top and a scroll/click to advance eras. Conveys the cinematic feel + clash risk for comparison.

title: Pass A mockup: diffusion backplate dithered into green-ASCII canvas
description: Quick pass of approach A. Reuse the tb303 brightness→ASCII-char sampling on a PLACEHOLDER source image (e.g. an existing og-*.png standing in for an SD era-scene). Show it as the live, mouse/audio-reactive green particle field so the user sees how an SD-generated scene becomes on-brand. Self-contained demo page in site/static/ (or a /passes/ scratch dir). Real technique, placeholder art.

title: StoryDiffusion × Canvas — visual direction passes (A/B/C)
description: Build quick visual passes of the three SD×Canvas integration approaches so the user can SEE each and pick a direction before committing. SD runs offline on ryuk's GPU (hosaka has none); site stays static. Passes use PLACEHOLDER source art (existing og-*.png / procedural) since real SD assets require the ryuk pipeline. Approaches: A=diffusion backplate dithered into the live green-ASCII canvas field (recommended, on-brand, reuses tb303 sampling tech); B=consistent-character journey morphing Moog→303 with canvas signal-flow overlay; C=comic/motion panels revealed on scroll with canvas accents. Enhancement layer on synths.html Visuals (c61.8) — does not block the core build.

title: synths: integration — index.html card+preview, sitemap, canvas-smoke test
description: Step 13. Add gallery card to site/static/index.html (mirror mirrorball/smiley at lines 193-207: a.card -> frame>canvas#synths-preview + .warn) AND a small preview renderer in the page animate() IIFE. Add /synths.html <url> to sitemap.xml (priority ~0.8). Add {name:'synths',path:'/synths.html',maxMeanMs:50} to PAGES in tests/canvas-smoke.spec.mjs. Add img/og-synths.png (placeholder ok). Run 'make test'. No build.py change.

title: synths: accessibility + perf pass (reduced-motion, no-autoplay, photosensitivity)
description: Step 12. prefers-reduced-motion: static frames, no audio auto-start, buttons still work. Photosensitivity review (no full-screen strobe; localized beat-rate luminance pulses). Confirm single rAF + single audio graph active at all times.

title: synths: Activation — IntersectionObserver one-active-zone + visibilitychange
description: Step 11. IntersectionObserver (threshold ~0.55) over 5 sections + rack. activate(zone) deactivates previous (cancel rAF, stop demo audio) before starting new -> exactly one rAF + one audio graph live (fixes tb303 stacked-canvas perf trap). visibilitychange -> cancel rAF + ctx.suspend() when hidden, resume only if transport was running. Never autoplay.

title: synths: RackUI — track rows, pattern selectors, transport, mute toggles
description: Step 10. Build bottom rack from ERAS: track rows (drums/bass/303), per-track pattern selectors, transport (play/stop/tempo), mute toggles to layer subsets into a song. Single shared Scheduler/master bus.

title: synths: EraSection factory — compose prose+canvas+play+links from ERAS x5
description: Step 9. EraSection(era) factory builds one section's DOM (prose, canvas, 'play this sound' button, Discogs links) + local audio wiring. Replicate across all 5 eras driven by ERAS[].

title: synths: Visuals — cached front-panel + live signal-flow overlay (one era)
description: Step 8. Per-frame composite: blit cached offscreen front-panel (redraw only on resize, a la tb303 draw303) + live signal-flow overlay (VCO/VCF/VCA boxes brighten with envelope/beat). Pulses localized, beat-rate, luminance-only within green family. dpr-aware resize. Build one era first.

title: synths: Controls — knob/slider abstraction bound to live AudioParams
description: Step 7. makeControl(el,{min,max,curve,onChange,format}): pointer-drag -> normalized [0,1] -> lin/exp value -> onChange writes param. Skinned <input type=range> for keyboard/SR a11y (aria-label/aria-valuetext), canvas knob overlay where retro look matters, touch-action:none. Bind cutoff/resonance/decay/accent/tempo; verify no zipper noise (setTargetAtValue ~0.01).

title: synths: Scheduler — Two-Clocks lookahead, multi-track sync
description: Step 6. setInterval(25ms) schedules ~0.1s ahead off ctx.currentTime; interval schedules only, all start/stop/ramp on audio clock. Shared 16-step clock for drums/bass/lead. Beat lights driven off scheduled-event queue (consumed when ctx.currentTime passes), not rAF. Confirm tight timing Chromium AND Firefox before layering; then add bass+lead, verify sync.
notes: Scheduler implemented: createTransport with two-clocks lookahead (25ms interval, 0.1s ahead), 16-step, mono+drum tracks, onStep hook for visuals. Era play buttons wired via toggleEraDemo (one demo audible at a time) + visibilitychange suspend. PENDING browser sync verification across Chromium+Firefox.

title: synths: DrumKit — synthesized kick/snare/hat (no samples)
description: Step 5. Pure Web Audio: kick (sine pitch-sweep 150->45Hz + click), snare (triangle body + bandpassed noise snap), hat (highpassed noise, closed/open decay). Cache one noise AudioBuffer at init. Test each trigger in isolation.
notes: DrumKit implemented: dkKick (sine pitch-sweep + click, 808/909 variants), dkSnare (triangle body + bandpassed noise), dkHat (highpassed noise, open/closed). Uses cached noiseBuffer. PENDING browser verification.

title: synths: 4-pole filter cascade + poles flag; tune Moog/Juno vs single-biquad 303
description: Step 4. preset.filter.poles flag: cascade two biquads (24dB/oct) for Moog/Juno/SH-101; single high-Q biquad for 303 (~18dB staggered diode-ladder character, cheaper). Tune by ear. No nonlinear AudioWorklet in v1 (future work). Also: 303 accent fans to 3 multipliers (VCA peak, env-mod, decay); slide = setTargetAtValue glide + legato.
notes: Filter poles flag implemented in makeVoice: poles>=4 cascades 2 biquads (Q split 0.4/1.0) for Moog/SH-101/Juno; poles==2 single high-Q biquad for 303. Presets set accordingly. PENDING by-ear tuning + browser verification.

title: synths: AudioEngine — lazy ctx, master+limiter, makeVoice (single-biquad first)
description: Step 3. Lazy AudioContext created only inside user gesture; master Gain -> DynamicsCompressor -> destination; {once:true} resume fallback. makeVoice(ctx,preset,dest): osc1/osc2+sub+noise -> oscMix -> lowpass VCF -> VCA, filterEnv on cutoff, ampEnv ADSR on VCA, routable LFO. Wire one 'play this sound' button; verify sound-on-gesture desktop + mobile.
notes: AudioEngine implemented in site/static/synths.html: lazy ensureAudio (gesture-gated), master Gain→DynamicsCompressor limiter, noteToFreq, makeVoice (osc+sub+noise→oscMix→VCF→VCA, ADSR amp+filter envs, LFO, glide, accent). Node syntax-checked. PENDING real-browser audible verification.

title: synths: author ERAS/PRESETS/PATTERNS content (prose, presets, 16-step patterns, Discogs links)
description: Step 2. Declarative ERAS[] (5 eras: id/year/title/blurb/preset/panel/demo/releases) driving both scroll sections and rack. Flat PRESETS{}, PATTERNS{} (all bass/lead in one scale e.g. A minor, drums complementary so any subset grooves), PANELS{}. Discogs RELEASE links per era. Pure data, no behavior. Audio embeds deferred.
close_reason: Authored ERAS[] (5 eras: prose, panel, demo, verified Discogs releases), PRESETS{} (moogLead/dubBass/sh101Bass/junoPad/acid303 with poles flags), PATTERNS{} (mono + drum, all A-minor/complementary), RACK_TRACKS. Release links render in DOM. Build+JS clean.

title: synths: scaffold static file (head/meta/favicon/JSON-LD, sections + rack shell)
description: Step 1. Create site/static/synths.html: head/meta, --bg/--primary CSS vars + font stack matching tb303.html, data-URI favicon, JSON-LD CreativeWork, OG/Twitter meta, back link, 5 empty <section class='era'> + <section id='rack'>. Confirm 'make build' produces output/synths.html and it serves.
close_reason: Scaffolded site/static/synths.html: head/meta/OG/JSON-LD, favicon, scrolling layout (hero + 5 data-driven era sections + rack shell + footer), IIFE skeleton with module banners. Builds clean, JS syntax-checked.

title: Synthesizer history page (synths.html) — scrollytelling + playable rack
description: Net-new self-contained site/static/synths.html: a scrollytelling history of synthesis (Moog -> dub/studio -> SH-101/Juno -> TR-808/909 -> TB-303) that teaches the subtractive signal path and culminates in a playable rack (drum machine + bass + 303) layering key-/time-locked patterns into a coherent track with live knobs/sliders. New Web Audio engine (nothing reusable in existing pages). Mirrors tb303.html conventions (single IIFE, CSS vars, data-URI favicon, dpr canvas, reduced-motion gating). One ERAS[] data structure drives both scroll sections and rack. Perf spine: IntersectionObserver keeps exactly one rAF + one audio graph live. Discogs release links per era; audio embeds/playable songs DEFERRED. Full plan: ~/.claude/plans/misty-fluttering-brook.md

title: Extract cymatics into standalone Chladni Plates piece, restore smiley purity
description:
The acid smiley now has a 4th 'cymatics' mode that auto-cycles through 6 genre-themed Chladni designs (house, techno, drum & bass, trance, acid, hip hop). Each design only gets ~7.2 s on screen before the crossfade — not enough time to really sit with a single resonance pattern. The cymatic work has outgrown its host: it dilutes the smiley's 1988-acid-house identity (op-art / zoetrope / rotsnake) and the curated patterns deserve a dedicated showcase.

Goal: split the cymatics work out into a standalone piece (proposed slug: chladni.html, title 'Chladni Plates') and return smiley.html to its 3-mode purity.
design:
STRUCTURE
- New file: site/static/chladni.html — full canvas piece, follows the shared conventions (back link, photo-warn if needed, prefers-reduced-motion gate, help pill, history modal explaining Chladni + the genre mappings).
- Curated examples instead of auto-loop: user picks among polished patterns with keyboard (e.g. 1-N or [ / ]), each pattern stays put indefinitely until cycled. Add an autoplay toggle if useful.
- Each pattern is hand-tuned (pulseHz, pulsePower, rotHz, field formula) for max visual impact — the current values are a decent first pass but each genre can be polished individually now that they don't need to share screen time.
- Preserve the ACID-letterform alignment as an optional feature (e.g. press A to trigger the snap), not auto-firing every 26 s.

LANDING PAGE
- Add a card to site/static/index.html linking to /chladni — match existing card aspect / frame / .warn slot pattern.
- Animated thumbnail showing one of the patterns rotating subtly.

REVERT IN smiley.html
- MODES back to ['op-art', 'zoetrope', 'rotsnake']
- Remove: CYM_DESIGNS array, CYM_* constants, renderCymaticsBg, buildAcidField, sampleAcidField, acidFieldData state, cymLastDesignIdx, cymDesignChangedAt, the call to buildAcidField() from init(), the cymatics dispatch in render(), the cymatics branch in staticMode.
- Update controls modal text to drop the cymatics description.
- Update meta description / OG / twitter / JSON-LD to drop 'Chladni-plate cymatic sand' phrasing.
- Keep SCRAMBLE_RADIUS at 140 (the larger mouse footprint applies to all modes — that part of this work stands on its own and the user explicitly asked for it across the board).
acceptance_criteria:
- smiley.html cycles only op-art / zoetrope / rotsnake (3 modes), no cymatic code paths remain.
- chladni.html exists and works as a standalone piece with manual pattern selection.
- chladni.html linked from index.html as a landing card with an animated thumbnail.
- Each pattern is well-tuned and visually distinct (no two designs look like neighbours).
- ACID alignment available as on-demand feature, not auto-loop.
- Photosensitivity warning + prefers-reduced-motion gate present on the new piece if it includes any pulsing chosen at high pulsePower.
- make build clean, make push deploys both files.
notes:
Source of truth for the existing 6 designs is the current smiley.html CYM_DESIGNS array. Reading from there is the fastest start for the new piece — copy the field functions verbatim, then iterate per design.

Bonus ideas for the new piece (skip if scope creep):
- Sound: optional WebAudio oscillators that play the genre's tempo as a subtle click track, sync'd to the visible pulse. Off by default.
- Frequency labels: each pattern displays a faux Hz number bottom-right (like the 5201 Hz on the resonance-experiment YouTube thumbnails the user referenced).
- Genre lineage timeline: hover to see year + region of origin.

title: Build acid smiley face particle page, swap into landing
description: Replace the TB-303 card on site/static/index.html with an acid-house smiley face particle showcase. New site/static/smiley.html with ASCII particles in a ☻ silhouette, drift/reassemble on mouse proximity, strobe pulse on S / 3-finger, slow wink/blink cycle. Keep tb303.html direct-accessible but off the landing page. Modal: brief smiley face / acid house cultural history.

title: Landing previews never pause when hidden or off-screen
description: site/static/index.html:489-496 runs two full animation loops (mirrorball preview + tb303 ASCII preview) regardless of tab visibility or card viewport position. Browsers throttle RAF to 1Hz when the tab is backgrounded but the previews still execute update logic. Gate rendering on document.visibilityState === 'visible' AND use IntersectionObserver to pause each preview when its card is off-screen (mobile scroll).

title: Physics explainer claims 'no Math.pow' but code uses it
description: mirrorball.html:324 in the modal says 'No Math.pow' as a perf choice, but mirrorball.html:863 calls Math.pow(NdotNegL, 1.7) in the per-facet hot loop. Either replace with the approximation n*sqrt(n)*sqrt(sqrt(n)) (which overshoots slightly — tune the coefficient), OR update the doc to say 'mostly no Math.pow — specular exponent uses it for accuracy'. Minor trust issue for the explainer modal.

title: Visitor counter burns RAF for a 100Hz update (retro pages)
description: site/templates/base.html.j2:43-58 animates the fake visitor counter via requestAnimationFrame — fires at 60Hz just to display a number advancing 100/sec. The counter is visible on every retro page (home, research). Replace with setInterval(tick, 100) to save ~59 frames/sec of wall-clock CPU and battery drain per page view. Functional behavior identical.

title: Per-facet gradient allocation creates GC pressure (mirrorball)
description: mirrorball.html:985 and mirrorball.html:1021 call ctx.createRadialGradient per facet per frame for halos — ~650 allocations/frame. Firefox's Canvas2D gradient object path is measurably slower than Chrome's. Current skip gate at line 982 is 'if (haloMinor > 0.5)' which fires for almost all dots. Change gate to brightness threshold (e.g., brightness > 0.15) to skip 30-50% of invisible halos. Low-brightness halos aren't visually distinguishable anyway.

title: Set up StoryDiffusion offline asset-gen on ryuk GPU
description: After a direction is chosen: stand up StoryDiffusion (SDXL, PyTorch/Gradio) on ryuk's idle GPU over the mesh (ben-laptop.personal.internal) for OFFLINE generation of consistent per-era assets. hosaka has no GPU. Supply-chain: pin model + deps, ≥14-day quarantine, confirm installs. Generated media committed as static assets; never in the deploy/build path. Document the gen step + prompts for reproducibility (not in user-facing copy per content-voice rule). Needs ryuk GPU model/VRAM check to pick SDXL vs lighter model + resolution.

title: Optimize tb303.html rendering perf on Firefox
description: tb303 Firefox averages ~22 fps (44 ms/frame) in headless Playwright — below the 30 fps gate used for other pages. Likely causes: ~20k fillText calls per frame across the ASCII particle grid; Firefox's text pipeline is slower than Chromium's. Options: (a) reduce particle density on Firefox, (b) batch fillText by color/alpha tiers, (c) cache a pre-rendered static chassis image and only redraw the mouse-scramble particles on top. Tracked via cross-browser test harness; currently the tb303 threshold is relaxed to 50 ms so the suite stays green.

title: Add type hints + ruff config to build.py
description: Python rule requires type hints on public interfaces and ruff for linting/formatting. site/build.py has neither. Add type hints and create pyproject.toml with [tool.ruff] section.

title: Debounce resize handler in mirrorball
description: mirrorball.html:459 attaches resize directly. Rapid resize events during window drag rebuild hazeCanvas/bloomCanvas/sibling buffers on each tick. Wrap in a 50-100ms trailing debounce.

title: Template naming is backwards (home vs index)
description: site/templates/home.html.j2 renders the home page (index.md). site/templates/index.html.j2 renders research/index.md. The naming is confusing — index.html.j2 suggests it's the index page. Swap names and update build.py:53-61 select_template() to match.

title: Gitignore .playwright-mcp/
description: Session git status shows .playwright-mcp/ as untracked. Add .playwright-mcp/ to .gitignore.

title: KaTeX CDN loaded without SRI integrity hashes
description: mirrorball.html:7 loads katex.min.css and mirrorball.html:1135-1136 load katex.min.js and auto-render.min.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net with crossorigin but no integrity="sha384-..." attribute. CDN compromise would execute arbitrary JS on the page. Pin the SRI hashes from the KaTeX release page.

title: Add make help target
description: Workstation rule requires a 'help' target listing available targets. Makefile has build/push/serve/dev/watch/open/clean but no help. Trivial: grep for ^[A-Za-z_-]+: in the Makefile and print description comments.

title: make watch orphans http.server on Ctrl-C
description: Makefile:26-29 backgrounds python3 -m http.server with & then runs an infinite find|entr loop. Ctrl-C breaks out of the loop but leaves the http.server process orphaned on DEV_PORT. Add 'trap "kill %1 2>/dev/null" EXIT INT TERM' before the background launch, or rework to use entr's -s shell mode.

title: No prefers-reduced-motion handling anywhere
description: Marquee (retro.css:55-62), visitor counter RAF, and all canvas animations ignore the user's OS-level reduced-motion setting. Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to retro.css to pause marquee. Check the media query in each canvas page's render loop to render a static frame instead of animating. Required for accessibility and by some corporate display policies.

title: Re-enable WebKit in Playwright matrix once libicu74 works
description: WebKit is installed in ~/.cache/ms-playwright/webkit-2272/ but fails to launch on Ubuntu 25.10 because minibrowser links against libicudata.so.74 and 25.10 ships libicu76 (not ABI-compatible). Current workaround: projects[] in playwright.config.mjs only adds webkit when PW_WEBKIT=1. Re-enable when one of: (a) Ubuntu provides libicu74-compat, (b) we test inside a pinned LTS container, (c) Playwright ships a WebKit build against 76. Not urgent — Chromium + Firefox cover the bulk of engine-divergence risk and were what the Firefox blur bug was about.

title: Google Fonts loaded without text= subsetting
description: site/templates/base.html.j2:9 loads Share Tech Mono full face. The site uses ~60 glyphs total. Add &text=... subset parameter or self-host the minimal subset.

title: extract_metadata blank-line handling is fragile
description: site/build.py:30-41 increments content_start on every blank line seen, even after meta keys parsed. Works only because downstream slice ignores them. Rewrite for clarity.

value: Session 2026-05-09 (hosaka): shipped cymatics mode for benjaminconnelly.com smiley — 6 genre-themed Chladni resonance designs (house/techno/drum & bass/trance/acid/hip hop) cycling on E-key 'cymatics', with per-design pulseHz, pulsePower (kick-shape), rotHz; ACID-letterform alignment every 26s; mouse SCRAMBLE_RADIUS bumped 70 → 140 across all modes. Deployed live (commit 9d2bdb5). Filed benjaminconnelly_com-jfc to extract this into a standalone Chladni Plates piece (chladni.html) with manual pattern selection — keeping smiley.html pure 3-mode op-art/zoetrope/rotsnake — that's the next move on this canvas.

Favicon across all site HTML entry points is a white Mitsubishi ecstasy pill (SVG data URI) — 3 black diamonds in classic Mitsubishi arrangement, black stroke around white circle. If updating the site's visual identity, remember to change all 5 files (base.html.j2 + index.html + mirrorball.html + tb303.html + smiley.html).

Content voice for user-facing pages: no meta-commentary about the site's own construction. That means no explaining why a particular icon was chosen for an effect, no comparisons between pages (e.g., 'a 303 falls apart at low sample rates'), no notes about sampling/rendering trade-offs. Visitors should read history, context, or story — never webmaster reasoning. Webmaster rationale belongs in commits, CLAUDE.md, or beads memories, not in modals or page copy.

Dev topology: hosaka is the canonical dev host (LAN 192.168.100.18, mesh hosaka.personal.internal). Per ADR-008 (~/src/personal/ops/decisions/008-hosaka-primary-dev-host.md), laptops are thin clients — Ryuk / ben-laptop (mesh ben-laptop.personal.internal, 100.113.179.77) holds only the Netbird daemon, terminal, and browser. No repos, no secrets on the laptop. All ~/src/personal/* paths, ~/dotfiles/, pass-store, AWS profiles, sops/age keys, and terraform state live on hosaka.

Implication for sessions: when running on hosaka and pointing the user at a service, do NOT give a localhost URL — the user is reading from their laptop. Use the Netbird IP (current wt0: 100.113.211.114) or the mesh hostname (hosaka.personal.internal). Only give 192.168.100.18 if the user is on the same LAN, which is rare. Authoritative inventory + topology lives at ~/src/personal/ops/inventory.md.

Live deploy process for benjaminconnelly.com: run `make push` from the repo root. It does three things: (1) python3 site/build.py → writes site/output/, (2) bd dolt push → syncs beads issue history to the Dolt remote, (3) ansible-playbook against ../benjaminconnelly-infra/ansible/playbooks/push-content.yml → rsyncs site/output/ to the EC2 webroot, fixes ownership, reloads nginx. Prerequisites: ../benjaminconnelly-infra clone must exist (override with INFRA_DIR=...), valid AWS creds in env, SSH key that reaches the EC2 host. WHEN to push: after every merged change to main that affects site/content/, site/static/, site/templates/, or css — the repo has no CI auto-deploy, so content doesn't reach benjaminconnelly.com until `make push` runs locally. Do NOT push a branch's WIP — only main-clean work (git pull --rebase clean first). Verify after push with curl -sI https://benjaminconnelly.com/{,smiley.html,mirrorball.html,tb303.html} — expect 200 on all. /research/ returning 401 is intentional (basic-auth protected).

Session 2026-04-16: benjaminconnelly.com shipped smiley page redesign — op-art ACID grid, hold-S chaos mode, E-key cycles through op-art/zoetrope/rotsnake illusions, touch gestures (1-finger strobe, 2-finger mode, pill buttons), per-segment ACID indexing, photosensitive warnings + prefers-reduced-motion gates across smiley + mirrorball. Cross-browser Playwright harness added (chromium + firefox pass; webkit gated on libicu74 — tracked in udn). Two open follow-ups: 68c (tb303 Firefox perf ~22fps) and udn (webkit re-enable). Last live deploy commit: 45ea763.
